data_in.description: task_003.expected_outputs[0]: Projected RT predictions for test set molecules (CSV table with molecule identifiers, predicted RTs, reference RTs)
METLIN small molecule dataset (SMRT) with 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular features
Pre-trained Bayesian meta-learning model (cond_meta_learning_10mol) or access to training code from constantino-garcia/cmmrt repository

data_out.description: Results table with error metrics (MAE, MdAE, and confidence intervals) comparing Bayesian meta-learning projection with 10 calibration molecules against baseline projection approaches

domain_knowledge: Bayesian meta-learning enables few-shot retention time projection across chromatographic methods by learning transferable weight distributions from limited calibration data.

Competitive error rates in this context mean achieving mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE) comparable to or better than existing baseline projection methods despite using only 10 calibration molecules.
MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, and Path Fingerprints are molecular encoding schemes that typically outperform raw chemical descriptors for retention time prediction tasks.
The METLIN small molecule dataset (SMRT) comprises 80,038 experimentally measured retention times and is the standard training corpus for retention time modeling in this work.

direct_checks: verify file exists in constantino-garcia/cmmrt repository containing trained cond_meta_learning_10mol model or checkpoint
verify script_runs: execute meta-learning model inference pipeline on a held-out test set of molecules with known retention times

verify output_matches_reference: mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE) from 10-molecule calibration set match or fall within reported competitive range against baseline projections
value_in_range: number of calibration molecules used in cond_meta_learning_10mol evaluation equals 10
file_format_is: model weights and hyperparameters are stored in standard format (HDF5, pickle, PT, or documented custom format)
contains_substring: repository documentation or paper supplementary materials explicitly reference 'cond_meta_learning_10mol' or equivalent model identifier
expert_review: assess whether reported error rates (MAE, MdAE) from 10-molecule meta-learning projection are statistically competitive with baseline projection approaches (e.g., linear regression, PLS, other meta-learning variants) when accounting for method differences and dataset characteristics
evaluate appropriateness of the 10-molecule calibration set selection: whether molecules are representative of chemical diversity and retention time range needed for reliable projection
judge whether comparison against baseline projections is fair and controlled (same training data, same test molecules, same evaluation metric definitions)

inputs: task_003.expected_outputs[0]: Projected RT predictions for test set molecules (CSV table with molecule identifiers, predicted RTs, reference RTs)
METLIN small molecule dataset (SMRT) with 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular features
Pre-trained Bayesian meta-learning model (cond_meta_learning_10mol) or access to training code from constantino-garcia/cmmrt repository
methodology_summary: Load the METLIN SMRT dataset (80,038 experimental retention times with pre-computed molecular descriptors and fingerprints).
Apply the Bayesian meta-learning projection model (cond_meta_learning_10mol) with 10 calibration molecules to project retention times across chromatographic methods.
Calculate mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE) between predicted and experimental retention times.
Tabulate results with confidence intervals and compare against baseline projection approach metrics (MAE ~39.2±1.2 s, MdAE ~17.2±0.9 s).

Validation: Verify that the Bayesian meta-learning approach achieves error rates competitive with (or better than) reported baseline projections while using only 10 calibration molecules, with MAE and MdAE within the confidence bounds reported for the original model.
missing_information: No changelog or version history found in discussion section

task_description: Using the Bayesian meta-learning projection approach (cond_meta_learning_10mol), reproduce the competitive error rates achieved with only 10 calibration molecules and compare against reported baseline projection methods. Output a results table with error metrics and method comparisons.

task_inputs.label: METLIN small molecule retention time dataset with molecular features

task_objective: Implement and validate the Bayesian meta-learning projection model with 10 calibration molecules, benchmarking its retention time prediction accuracy against baseline chromatographic method transfer approaches.

task_outputs.label: Results table with MAE, MdAE, and confidence intervals for Bayesian meta-learning vs. baseline methods

title: Reproduce meta-learning projection competitive error rates with 10-molecule calibration sets
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load the METLIN small molecule dataset (SMRT) containing 80,038 experimental retention times and corresponding molecular features (5,666 descriptors and 2,214 fingerprints generated via alvaDesc). 2. Apply the Bayesian meta-learning projection model (cond_meta_learning_10mol) trained with 10 calibration molecules to project retention times across chromatographic methods. 3. Evaluate projected retention times by calculating mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE) against ground-truth experimental values. 4. Tabulate error metrics and compare against baseline projection approach results reported in the paper (expected MAE ~39.2±1.2 s, MdAE ~17.2±0.9 s).
